title: Agada Tantra Case Proforma
subtitle: NCISM — Toxicology, Forensic Medicine & Medico-Legal (Visha Chikitsa)

sections.title: Visha Pariksha — Poisoning / Toxin Exposure Details

fields.content: ⚠ MEDICO-LEGAL: All suspected poisoning cases are MLC (Medico-Legal Cases) by law. Register MLC immediately. Police intimation is mandatory for criminal/intentional cases (IPC §304A/§328). Preserve gastric washings/blood as medico-legal specimens.

fields.label: Visha type (Ayurveda)
fields.hint: Garavisha = adulterated / intentional slow poison — CRIMINAL — mandatory police reporting

fields.label: Time of exposure (note for stomach emptying window)
fields.hint: Gastric lavage effective within 1 hour. Activated charcoal effective up to 2–4 hours

fields.label: Quantity ingested/exposed (approx.)
fields.placeholder: e.g. ~30 tablets, 500 mL bottle, one bite on left hand

fields.label: Name of substance (if known)
fields.placeholder: Trade name / generic name / plant name (Sanskrit + common) / chemical name…

sections.title: Prathama Chikitsa — First Response & Emergency Management

fields.content: ⚠ Priority sequence: Airway → Breathing → Circulation → Decontamination → Antidote. Stabilise patient FIRST before any Ayurveda intervention. Note: Vamana (emesis) is CONTRAINDICATED in corrosive/caustic ingestion and in unconscious patient.

fields.label: Gastric lavage (Udarda Shodhana)
fields.hint: Contraindicated: corrosive ingestion, petroleum products, convulsing patient, no airway protection

fields.label: Antidote (Visha Nashaka) administered
fields.placeholder: OP poison = Atropine + Pralidoxime; Paracetamol = NAC; Opioid = Naloxone; Digoxin = Digibind; None
fields.hint: Antidote guide: OP = Atropine 2mg IV q10min (titrate to drying secretions). Snake = ASV (Anti-snake Venom)

fields.content: ℹ Toxidrome recognition is the fastest way to identify poison class when name is unknown. Cholinergic (OP/carbamate) = SLUDGE: Salivation, Lacrimation, Urination, Defaecation, GI cramps, Emesis. Anticholinergic = dry as bone, red as beet, hot as hare, blind as bat, mad as hatter.

fields.label: Level of consciousness
fields.hint: GCS ≤8 = intubate + ventilate. Never leave unattended

fields.label: GCS score (E+V+M)
fields.hint: E: 1–4; V: 1–5; M: 1–6. Normal = 15. GCS 8 = need intubation. GCS 3 = deep coma

fields.label: Pupils
options: Fixed dilated (severe CNS — very poor prognosis)
fields.hint: Miosis + bradycardia + excessive secretions = OP poisoning emergency

fields.label: Breathing pattern
fields.hint: Bronchospasm + excessive salivation + miosis = OP/carbamate (cholinergic crisis)

fields.label: Skin signs
options: Bite/sting mark — site + dimensions (note for ASV decision)

fields.label: Toxidrome pattern (most closely matches)
options: Anticholinergic — dry, hot, red, blind, mad (Dhatura/Atropine/TCAs)
Opioid syndrome — respiratory depression, miosis, coma (Morphine/Heroin)
Serotonin syndrome — tremor, clonus, hyperthermia, agitation
fields.hint: Match toxidrome + route + history to narrow poison class when name unknown

fields.content: ⚠ LEGAL DUTY: All poisoning cases are MLC. Incomplete or delayed MLC documentation can have criminal/civil consequences. Document everything contemporaneously. Do not erase or overwrite.

fields.label: MLC case registered?
options: To be decided by doctor in charge

fields.label: Police intimation
options: Not yet done — do immediately for criminal cases

fields.label: Psychiatric consultation (intentional cases)
fields.hint: Mandatory for all intentional self-harm. Mental Health Act 2017: patient retains right to refuse treatment once competent
